title: quercus × bebbiana
intro: Quercus × bebbiana, also known as Bebb's white oak, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The bark and leaves of this plant contain tannins, which have been used for their medicinal properties, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus × bebbiana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Quercus × bebbiana include infusions and teas, which can be made by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water, and can be used to alleviate various health issues.
